source_pr.title: lightning: improve the performance of duplicate detection
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/29588

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Improve the performance of duplicate detection by implementing concurrency logic to collecting duplicates and resolving duplicates. I tested about 900 million rows duplicates. The cost time is reduced from serval hours to 5m30s(detection 3m30s, resolution 1m35s) on my Ryzen 7 4800H laptop.


This PR is too large 🤣. It has been split into multiple small PRs:
- [x] https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/29975
- [ ] https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/31006

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: sort.Slice(dupPairs, func(i, j int) bool { (br/pkg/lightning/backend/local/iterator_test.go).
